name: Spaghetti Carbonara
instructions:
1. Cook spaghetti according to package instructions.
2. Fry pancetta until crispy.
3. Beat eggs with grated parmesan and pepper.
4. Drain pasta, mix with pancetta, then quickly stir in egg mixture off heat.
5. Serve immediately with extra parmesan.

name: Chocolate Chip Cookies
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
2. Cream butter and sugars until fluffy.
3. Beat in eggs one at a time, then add vanilla.
4. Mix in flour, then stir in chocolate chips.
5. Drop spoonfuls onto baking sheets and bake for 9-11 minutes.

name: Pork Sausage Cavatappi Bolognese
instructions:
1
• Adjust rack to top position and preheat oven to 450 degrees. Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Wash and dry produce. • Trim and halve zucchini lengthwise; slice crosswise into 1⁄2-inch-thick half-moons. Halve, peel, and thinly slice onion.

Roast Zucchini
2
• Toss zucchini on a baking sheet with a drizzle of oil, half the Italian Seasoning (you’ll use the rest later), and a pinch of salt and pepper. • Roast on top rack, tossing halfway through, until browned and tender, 14-16 minutes. • Tent with foil to keep warm.

Cook Pasta
3
• Once water is boiling, add cavatappi to pot. Cook, stirring occasionally, until al dente, 9-11 minutes. • Reserve 11⁄2 cups pasta cooking water (2 cups for 4 servings), then drain.

Cook Onion & Sausage
4
• While pasta cooks, remove sausage* from casing if necessary; discard casing. • Heat a drizzle of oil in a large pan over medium-high heat. Add onion and a pinch of salt and pepper. Cook, stirring occasionally, until softened, 5-6 minutes. • Add sausage and cook, breaking up meat into pieces, until browned, 3-4 minutes (it’ll finish cooking in the next step).

Make Sauce
5
• Add tomato paste and remaining Italian Seasoning to pan. Cook, stirring constantly, until fragrant, 1 minute. • Stir in stock concentrate, 1 cup reserved pasta cooking water (11⁄2 cups for 4 servings), and a big pinch of salt and pepper. Bring to a simmer; cook until sauce has slightly thickened and sausage is cooked through, 2-3 minutes. • Reduce heat to medium low.

Finish & Serve
6
• Stir drained cavatappi, roasted zucchini, sour cream, and 1 TBSP butter (2 TBSP for 4 servings) into pan until thoroughly combined. TIP: If needed, stir in more reserved cooking water a splash at a time until pasta is coated in a creamy sauce. • Divide pasta between bowls. Sprinkle with Parmesan and serve.

Pork Sausage is fully cooked when internal temperature reaches 160°.

name: One-Pan Cheesy Chicken Tortilla Melts
instructions:
1. Wash and dry produce. Halve, core, and thinly slice green pepper into strips. In a small microwave-safe bowl, combine sour cream, cream cheese, 1 tsp water, and hot sauce to taste. Microwave until softened, 30 seconds. Stir to combine.

2. Heat a drizzle of oil in a large pan over medium-high heat. Add green pepper and cook, stirring occasionally, until slightly softened, 2–3 minutes.

3. Add chicken to pan with green pepper. Season with salt and pepper. Cook, stirring occasionally, until chicken is browned and cooked through and green pepper is tender, 4–6 minutes. Stir in stock concentrate until combined. Turn off heat.

4. Place tortillas on a clean work surface. Spread one half of each tortilla with half the spicy cream sauce (save the rest for serving). Using a slotted spoon, top with chicken filling, then sprinkle with cheddar. Fold tortillas in half.

5. Wipe out pan. Melt 2 TBSP butter over medium heat. Add tortilla melts; cook in batches until golden brown and cheese is melted, 3–4 minutes per side. Transfer to paper towel-lined cutting board.

6. Cut tortilla melts into wedges. Serve with remaining spicy cream sauce on the side for dipping.

name: One-Pan Cheesy Beef Tortilla Melts
instructions:
1. Wash and dry produce. Halve, core, and thinly slice bell pepper into strips. In a small microwave-safe bowl, combine sour cream, cream cheese, 1 tsp water, and hot sauce to taste. Microwave until softened, 30 seconds. Stir to combine.

2. Heat a drizzle of oil in a large pan over medium-high heat. Add bell pepper and cook, stirring occasionally, until slightly softened, 2–3 minutes.

3. Add beef to pan with bell pepper. Season with salt and pepper. Cook, breaking up meat into pieces, until beef is browned and cooked through and bell pepper is tender, 3–4 minutes. Drain any excess grease. Stir in stock concentrate. Turn off heat.

4. Place tortillas on a clean work surface. Spread one half of each with half the cream sauce (save the rest for serving). Top with beef filling and cheddar. Fold tortillas in half.

5. Wipe out pan. Melt 2 TBSP butter over medium heat. Cook tortilla melts in batches until golden brown and cheese is melted, 3–4 minutes per side. Transfer to paper towel-lined cutting board.

6. Cut melts into wedges and serve with remaining cream sauce on the side.

name: One Pot Spicy Rigatoni alla Rossa
instructions:
1. Heat a large drizzle of olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add tomato paste, Italian seasoning, garlic powder, stock concentrate, and chili flakes to taste. Cook, stirring, until fragrant and tomato paste darkens, 30–60 seconds. Whisk in cream cheese until melted and combined, 20–30 seconds.

2. Stir in rigatoni and 2 cups water. Season with salt and pepper. Bring to a simmer. Cook, stirring constantly, until rigatoni is tender and sauce is creamy and thickened, 5–6 minutes. Remove from heat. Add spinach and stir until wilted, 30–60 seconds. (Add in batches if needed.)

3. Tear mozzarella into bite-size pieces and sprinkle over pasta. Season with salt and pepper. Serve in shallow bowls with extra chili flakes on the side.

name: Cheesy Baked Pasta with Meat Sauce
instructions:
1. Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Add penne and cook until al dente, about 8 minutes. Reserve 1 cup pasta water, then drain and set aside.

2. Preheat broiler to high. Grate garlic and tear mozzarella into ½-inch pieces. In a large ovenproof skillet, heat olive oil over medium-high. Add beef, salt, and pepper; cook until browned and no longer pink, 3–5 minutes.

3. Stir garlic into beef; cook 1 minute. Add marinara and reserved pasta water. Simmer briefly, then stir in cooked pasta. Season to taste.

4. Top with mozzarella. Broil until cheese is melted and bubbling, 2–3 minutes. Serve hot.

name: Pub-Style Shepherd’s Pie
instructions:
1. Heat broiler to high. Dice potatoes into ½-inch pieces. Prep thyme, carrot, celery, and onion.

2. Boil potatoes in salted water until tender, 15–20 minutes. Drain, return to pot, and mash with sour cream, 1 tsp thyme, and 2 TBSP butter. Season with salt and pepper. Cover and set aside.

3. Heat oil in ovenproof pan over medium-high. Add carrot; cook 2–3 minutes. Add celery, onion, and more oil; cook until tender, 5–7 minutes. Stir in garlic powder and 2 tsp thyme; cook 30 seconds.

4. Add ground beef; season and cook until browned, 4–6 minutes. Stir in flour; cook 1 minute.

5. Add crushed tomatoes and ½ cup water. Stir in stock concentrates. Bring to a boil and cook until thickened, 1–2 minutes. Season to taste. (Transfer to baking dish if pan is not ovenproof.)

6. Spread mashed potatoes on top, leaving a gap around the edges. Sprinkle cheddar over top. Broil 3–4 minutes until browned. Serve hot.

name: Beef & Zucchini Baked Penne
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 425°F. Boil water. Quarter zucchini lengthwise, slice into quarter-moons.

2. Cook penne until al dente, 9–11 minutes. Reserve 1 cup pasta water, drain, and set aside.

3. In a large pan, heat oil. Add beef, zucchini, Italian seasoning, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Cook until beef is done and zucchini is tender, 5–7 minutes. Drain grease if needed.

4. In the pasta pot, combine beef mixture, marinara, tomato paste, stock concentrate, and ½ cup pasta water. Boil 2–3 min until thickened. Stir in penne. Season to taste.

5. Transfer to a greased 9x13 dish. Dollop with ricotta, sprinkle with mozzarella. Bake 10–12 minutes until melted, then broil 2–3 minutes until golden.

6. Let cool 5 minutes. Top with chili flakes and serve.

name: Cavatappi Beef Ragù
instructions:
1. Boil a pot of salted water. Peel and finely chop carrot. Roughly chop parsley leaves.

2. Add cavatappi and cook until al dente, 9–11 min. Drain and set aside.

3. In a pan, heat oil and cook carrot until browned and tender, 6–8 min. Season with salt and pepper.

4. Push carrot to the side. Add more oil, then beef. Brown and break up beef, season with Italian seasoning, salt, and pepper. Combine with carrot.

5. Add marinara and ⅓ cup water. Simmer 3–4 min. Stir in cream cheese. Add pasta, butter, and half the parsley. Stir to coat. Adjust seasoning.

6. Serve in bowls topped with Parmesan and remaining parsley.

name: Beef Flautas Supreme
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 425°F and line a baking sheet with foil. Thinly slice onion, then mince a bit for pico later.

2. In a pan, cook sliced onion in oil 3 min. Add beef and Southwest Spice; cook until browned, 4–6 min. Stir in Tex-Mex paste and water; simmer 2–4 min. Off heat.

3. Rub tortillas with olive oil. Fill with beef and cheese, roll tightly into flautas. Place seam-down on sheet. Bake 8–12 min until crisp.

4. Dice tomato. In a bowl, combine tomato, minced onion, lime zest, and juice. In another bowl, mix sour cream with zest and juice; thin with water. Serve flautas with pico and crema.

name: One-Pan Trattoria Tortelloni Bake
instructions:
1. Preheat broiler. Mince garlic, dice tomatoes. In a bowl, mix panko, Parmesan, olive oil, salt, and pepper.

2. In an ovenproof pan, heat olive oil. Add garlic, Italian Seasoning, chili flakes; cook 30 sec. Add tomatoes, salt, pepper; cook 1–2 min. Stir in tomato paste, then add water, stock, and cream cheese. Simmer until smooth.

3. Stir in tortelloni; cook 4–6 min until tender. Stir in butter. Adjust consistency with water if needed. (Transfer to baking dish if needed.)

4. Top with panko mix. Broil 2–4 min until golden. Serve with chili flakes if desired.

name: Chicken Thyme with Zucchini Couscous
instructions:
1. Dice zucchini, garlic, tomato, scallions, and zest the lemon. Quarter lemon.

2. Sauté zucchini with garlic, salt, pepper in oil until tender. Set aside.

3. Toast couscous with garlic and butter. Add water and salt, simmer until tender.

4. Season chicken with thyme, salt, pepper. Cook in zucchini pan until browned and cooked through. Rest under foil.

5. In same pan, cook tomato and scallion whites in butter. Add stock and water, reduce. Remove from heat, whisk in cream cheese.

6. Slice chicken. Mix couscous with zucchini, scallion greens, lemon zest and juice. Plate couscous, top with chicken and sauce. Serve with lemon wedges.

name: Beef Bulgogi Meatballs
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 425°F. Peel and slice carrots; roast on baking sheet with oil, salt, and pepper until tender, 20–25 minutes.

2. Peel and mince ginger. Slice scallions, separating whites and greens.

3. Sauté half the ginger in oil; add rice, water, and salt. Simmer until tender, 15–18 minutes.

4. Mix beef, scallion whites, panko, remaining ginger, and some bulgogi sauce. Form into meatballs and bake 14–16 min.

5. Mix sour cream with sriracha and water until drizzle consistency. Toss cooked meatballs in remaining bulgogi sauce.

6. Plate rice with carrots and meatballs. Drizzle crema on top and garnish with scallion greens and sesame seeds.

name: Firecracker Meatballs
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 425°F. Slice scallions (whites/minced, greens/garnish). Mince ginger.

2. In a bowl, mix mayo, sour cream, honey, 2 tsp soy sauce, and sriracha to taste for sauce.

3. Toast half the sesame seeds in oil, then add rice, water, and salt. Simmer covered 15–18 min.

4. In another bowl, mix beef, panko, ginger, scallion whites, remaining soy sauce, salt, and pepper. Form into meatballs.

5. Place meatballs and green beans (with oil, salt, pepper) on a baking sheet. Roast 14–16 min.

6. Fluff rice. Toss meatballs in firecracker sauce. Plate rice, meatballs, and green beans. Garnish with scallion greens, sesame seeds, and chili flakes to taste.

name: Sweet Chili Beef & Green Bean Bowls
instructions:
1. Cook rice in salted water until tender, 15-18 min. Set aside.

2. Trim and chop green beans, quarter lime, chop cilantro.

3. Brown ground beef in oil, 3-4 min undisturbed, then break up and cook through. Drain excess grease if needed.

4. Steam green beans in microwave with splash of water (1-2 min) or on stovetop (5-7 min).

5. Add green beans to beef along with chili sauce, 2½ TBSP sweet soy glaze, ½ tsp sugar, and 1 TBSP butter. Cook 1-2 min. Finish with lime juice.

6. Fluff rice, stir in 1 TBSP butter, season. Serve rice topped with beef, onions, cilantro, and lime wedges.

name: Mozzarella & Herb Chicken
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 425°F. Wash produce. Peel and cut carrots diagonally. Zest and quarter lemon.

2. Mix panko, mozzarella, 1 tsp Italian seasoning, 1 TBSP olive oil, salt, and pepper. Toss carrots with oil, salt, pepper, and chili flakes. Roast carrots on top rack for 5 min.

3. Pat chicken dry; season. Spread sour cream on top and press panko mix on top (not bottom).

4. Add chicken to baking sheet. Roast 15-20 min until cooked through. Remove carrots early if done.

5. Toast couscous in butter, then add water and stock concentrate. Simmer 6-8 min until tender.

6. Finish couscous with butter, lemon zest, and juice. Season. Plate chicken, carrots, and couscous. Serve with lemon wedges.

name: Turkey Ragù Spaghetti
instructions:
1. Bring salted water to a boil. Wash produce. Grate zucchini.

2. Cook spaghetti until al dente, 9–11 min. Reserve ¾ cup pasta water, then drain.

3. Heat oil in pan. Cook turkey, Tuscan Heat Spice, garlic powder, salt & pepper until browned. Add zucchini and cook 2–3 min. Add marinara, stock, and pasta water; simmer 1–2 min. Lower heat and stir in cream cheese, sour cream, half the Parmesan, and butter. Season to taste.

4. Toss spaghetti into sauce. Add more water if needed. Plate and top with remaining Parmesan.

name: Italian Sausage Rigatoni
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 450°F. Remove sausage casing if needed. In oven tray, combine sausage (broken up), rigatoni, peas, romesco, stock concentrate, and 1¼ cups hot water. Stir to mix. 

2. Cover tray tightly with foil and bake 20 min. Uncover, stir gently, and bake another 12–15 min until sausage is cooked and pasta is al dente. 

3. Top with Parmesan and serve.

name: Pork Sausage Rigatoni Rosa
instructions:
1. Boil salted water and cook rigatoni 9–11 min until al dente. Reserve 1 cup pasta water, then drain. Slice zucchini into half-moons and mince garlic. 

2. Sauté zucchini in oil 5–6 min. Remove. Cook sausage in same pan until almost done. 

3. Add garlic and tomato paste; cook 1–2 min. Stir in cream cheese, stock, ½ cup pasta water, butter, sugar, and chili flakes. Mix until smooth. 

4. Add pasta and zucchini. Stir in half the Parmesan. Add more water as needed for creamy texture. Top with remaining Parmesan and serve.

name: Turkey & Queso Nacho Crunch Burritos
instructions:
1. Cook rice in salted water until tender, about 15 minutes. Keep covered off heat.

2. Dice tomato. Slice scallions, separating whites and greens. Quarter lime.

3. Heat oil in a large pan. Add ground turkey, scallion whites, and Southwest Spice Blend. Cook, breaking up meat, until browned and cooked through.

4. Warm queso in a small bowl. Crush tortilla chips slightly for crunch.

5. Stir lime zest and juice into rice along with scallion greens. Season to taste.

6. Warm tortillas. Fill each with rice, turkey mixture, tomato, queso, and crushed chips.

7. Roll into burritos, slice if desired, and serve with extra lime wedges.

name: Chicken Sausage and Spinach Ravioli
instructions:
1. Bring salted water to a boil. Slice scallions (separate whites and greens), dice tomato, quarter lemon.

2. Heat oil in large pan. Add scallion whites, sausage, and Italian seasoning. Cook 3–5 min until browned. Stir in tomato and cook 1–2 min.

3. Cook ravioli in boiling water 4–5 min until floating. Reserve ½ cup pasta water, then drain well.

4. Stir stock concentrate, lemon juice, and splash of pasta water into sausage pan. Simmer 1–2 min. Stir in sour cream and butter. Season. Add more pasta water if needed for creaminess.

5. Gently stir ravioli into sauce. Adjust salt, pepper, and lemon to taste.

6. Divide into bowls and garnish with scallion greens. Serve with extra lemon wedges.

name: Creamy Chicken & Bacon Spaghetti
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F. Cut tomatoes into wedges. Wrap garlic in foil with olive oil, salt, and pepper. Roast tomatoes (with Tuscan Heat Spice) and garlic 20–25 minutes.

2. Cook bacon in a pan until crispy, 6–10 minutes. Remove and chop.

3. Boil salted water and cook spaghetti 9–11 minutes until al dente. Reserve ¾ cup pasta water, then drain.

4. Season chicken with Tuscan Heat Spice, salt, and pepper. Cook in oil 3–5 minutes per side until cooked through. Keep warm.

5. In pasta pot, melt butter. Mash roasted garlic with Tuscan Heat Spice. Stir in reserved pasta water, cream cheese, half the Parmesan, and more butter until smooth.

6. Toss spaghetti in sauce until coated. Plate with sliced chicken and roasted tomatoes. Top with bacon and remaining Parmesan.

name: Garlic Butter Shrimp Scampi
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 450°F. Cut broccoli into florets. Zest and quarter lemon. Mince garlic.

2. Toss broccoli with olive oil, salt, and pepper. Roast 12–15 minutes until crispy.

3. Cook linguine in salted boiling water 9–11 minutes until al dente. Reserve ½ cup pasta water, then drain.

4. Soften butter and mix with lemon zest, half the Parmesan, a pinch of garlic, and chili flakes.

5. Pat shrimp dry. Toss with olive oil, remaining garlic, salt, and pepper. Cook in a hot pan 2–4 minutes until opaque.

6. Add pasta, broccoli, stock concentrate, garlic butter, and reserved pasta water to shrimp. Toss until coated. Finish with lemon juice, salt, and pepper. Serve topped with remaining Parmesan and chili flakes.

name: Garlicky Shrimp Couscous Bowls
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 450°F. Cut broccoli if needed. Mince garlic, chop parsley, quarter lemon.

2. Toss broccoli with olive oil, chili flakes, salt, and pepper. Roast 12–15 minutes.

3. In a small pot, sauté half the garlic in olive oil. Add couscous, stock concentrate, water, and salt. Simmer covered 6–8 minutes until tender.

4. Pat shrimp dry. Cook in olive oil with garlic powder, salt, and pepper 3–4 minutes. Add water and cook until evaporated. Lower heat; add butter, remaining garlic, chili flakes. Toss with half the parsley and lemon juice.

5. Fluff couscous. Stir in remaining parsley, lemon juice, and olive oil. Season.

6. Divide couscous into bowls. Top with shrimp and broccoli. Spoon garlic butter over and serve with lemon wedges.

name: Lemony Chicken & Grains Spinach Salad
instructions:
1. Heat oil in a large pan over medium-high. Add chicken and cook until beginning to brown, 2–4 minutes.

2. Separate grain blend in package, then add to pan with chicken along with 2 TBSP water. Cover and cook until warmed through and chicken is fully cooked, 2–4 minutes. Season with salt and pepper.

3. In a large bowl, whisk together green goddess dressing, yogurt, olive oil, salt, and pepper.

4. Add spinach and chicken-grain mixture to bowl; toss to coat. Divide between bowls and sprinkle with feta.

name: Mushroom Ravioli in a Tomato Cream Sauce
instructions:
1. Bring a medium pot of salted water to a boil. Halve large tomatoes. Stir ricotta with olive oil, salt, and pepper.

2. Heat olive oil in a large pan. Add tomatoes and Italian seasoning; cook 2–3 minutes. Stir in stock concentrate, marinara, cream cheese, and water. Simmer until thickened.

3. Cook ravioli in boiling water until tender and floating, 3–4 minutes. Reserve ¼ cup pasta water, then drain.

4. Add ravioli and butter to sauce; toss to coat. Add reserved pasta water as needed for creaminess. Season.

5. Divide between bowls and top with a dollop of ricotta.

name: One-Pan Baja Chicken Quesadillas
instructions:
1. Dice onion. Pat chicken dry.

2. Heat oil in a large pan. Cook chicken with blackening spice, onion, salt, and pepper until browned and cooked through, 4–6 minutes. Remove and wipe pan.

3. Make salsa: combine diced tomato, lime juice, reserved onion, salt, and pepper.

4. Make lime crema: mix sour cream, lime juice, zest, and a splash of water to thin. Season.

5. Assemble quesadillas: layer cheeses and chicken on half of each tortilla; fold. Cook in butter and oil until golden and crispy, 1–2 minutes per side.

6. Slice and serve with salsa, lime crema, hot sauce, and lime wedges.

name: One-Pan Cantina Shrimp Fajitas
instructions:
1. Slice poblano and onion; quarter lime. Mix guacamole with lime juice and a dash of hot sauce.

2. Heat oil in a large pan. Cook poblano and onion until softened, 4–5 minutes. Add half the fajita spice and cook 30 seconds.

3. Pat shrimp dry. Push veggies aside; add oil and shrimp with remaining spice. Cook until opaque, 3–4 minutes. Combine with veggies and squeeze lime over top.

4. Warm tortillas. Fill with shrimp mixture, top with guacamole and crema, and serve with hot sauce and lime wedges.

name: Salmon in a Creamy Dijon Chive Sauce
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 450°F. Cut potatoes into wedges and zucchini into half-moons. Zest and quarter lemon; mince chives.

2. Toss potatoes with olive oil, salt, and pepper. Roast 10 minutes.

3. Toss zucchini with olive oil, salt, and pepper. Add to baking sheet and roast 12–15 minutes more until potatoes are golden and zucchini tender.

4. Pat salmon dry; season with salt and pepper. Heat butter and olive oil in a pan. Cook salmon skin-side down 5–7 minutes, flip and cook 1–2 minutes more. Remove and wipe pan.

5. In same pan, add stock concentrate, Dijon, lemon juice, and water. Simmer 2–3 minutes. Remove from heat; stir in sour cream, half the chives, and butter. Adjust seasoning.

6. Toss zucchini with lemon zest. Serve salmon with potatoes and zucchini, drizzle with sauce, and garnish with remaining chives.

name: Shrimp Spaghetti with a Kick
instructions:
1. Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Cook spaghetti until al dente, 9–11 minutes. Reserve ½ cup pasta water, then drain.

2. Slice zucchini and chili. Pat shrimp dry.

3. Heat olive oil in a large pan. Cook zucchini and chili 2–3 minutes until lightly browned.

4. Push zucchini aside; add more oil and cook shrimp 3–4 minutes until opaque.

5. Add drained spaghetti, garlic herb butter, half the Parmesan, and reserved pasta water to the pan. Toss until coated, adding more pasta water if needed. Season.

6. Divide between bowls. Top with remaining Parmesan and a drizzle of olive oil. Garnish with extra chili if desired.

name: White Cheddar Crunch Burgers
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 450°F. Cut potatoes into wedges, toss with oil, salt, and pepper, and roast 20–25 minutes until golden and tender.

2. Slice onion into rounds and halve buns.

3. Heat oil in a pan and cook onion rounds until browned and tender, 2–4 minutes per side. Remove and wipe pan.

4. Form beef into two patties. Season with salt and pepper. Cook 3–5 minutes per side to desired doneness. In final 1–2 minutes, top with cheddar and cover to melt.

5. Toast buns and spread smoky mustard on cut sides.

6. Assemble burgers with patties, griddled onions, and crispy fried onions. Serve with roasted potato wedges.

name: Rigatoni with Beef & Zucchini Ragù
instructions:
1. Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Cook rigatoni until al dente, 9–11 minutes. Reserve 1 cup pasta water, then drain.

2. Prep: Dice shallot. Mince/grate garlic. Grate zucchini on a box grater, then squeeze out as much liquid as possible (kitchen towel bundle or press in a strainer).

3. Heat a drizzle of oil in a large pan over medium-high. Add ground beef, shallot, Italian seasoning, salt, and pepper. Cook 3–5 minutes, breaking up meat, until browned and mostly cooked.

4. Add tomato paste; cook 1–2 minutes, stirring, until combined and beef is cooked through.

5. Add grated zucchini, garlic, and a big pinch of salt; cook 2–3 minutes until zucchini is tender.

6. Stir in cream cheese, stock concentrate, ½ cup reserved pasta water, and ½ tsp sugar. Simmer 1–2 minutes until slightly thickened. Season with salt and pepper.

7. Reduce heat to medium-low. Stir in drained rigatoni, half the Parmesan, and 1 TBSP butter until combined. Add more pasta water as needed to loosen.

8. Serve topped with remaining Parmesan.
